- Germany: The discreet lives of the super rich | DW Documentary: Kind: captions Language: id Jerman super kaya. Tidak ada negara Eropa lain yang memiliki miliarder sebanyak Jerman. Dan sementara kekayaan mereka bertambah, semakin banyak orang Jerman hidup di bawah garis kemiskinan yang ditetapkan Organisasi untuk Kerjasama Ekonomi dan Pembangunan OECD. Pers sering melaporkan tingginya ketidaksetaraan pendapatan negara itu dan mobilitas sosial yang rendah. Tetapi sedikit yang diketahui tentang orang super kaya. Tentu uang menarik sukses dan sukses menarik uang. Saya percaya itu. Saya telah alami sendiri bagaimana Anda tiba-tiba bisa terhubung dengan orang-orang yang sebelumnya tidak terjangkau. Siapakah orang-orang super kaya Jerman? Bagaimana mereka hidup? Dan bagaimana mereka melihat negara tempat mereka tinggal? Cara orang-orang kaya di Jerman dibicarakan mengganggu saya, karena itu sensasional dan memalukan, dan sama sekali tidak mencerminkan apa yang telah dilakukan orang kaya, dan terus dilakukan, untuk negara ini. Ini menciptakan kesan orang kaya seperti Paman Gober Bebek. Bahwa mereka punya gudang uang tempat mereka berendam dalam koin mereka. Paman Gober ingin lebih, lebih, lebih banyak! Itu bukan dunia saya. Saya suka mendapatkan uang, tapi bukan berenang di dalamnya. Kami ingin menilik lebih dekat dunia terpisah orang ultrakaya di Jerman - para pemilik dan pewaris perusahaan bernilai miliaran. Kami ingin mengetahui apa yang memotivasi mereka yang berada di peringkat puncak dunia. PERINGKAT PUNCAK DUNIA Kehidupan Tersendiri Para Super Kaya Film karya Florian Opitz Setiap tahun sebuah acara eksklusif berlangsung di Schlosshotel Kronberg dekat Frankfurt, di mana publik tidak diundang. Itu adalah malam "Hall of Fame" tahunan, untuk majalah bulanan Manager Magazine. Hampir tidak ada acara lain di Jerman yang menarik begitu banyak pemilik bisnis kaya. Para moderator yang terhormat, anggota dewan juri yang baik, hadirin yang terhormat, Selamat datang di Hall of Fame Manager Magazine. Ketika kami pertama kali membentuk Hall of Fame pada tahun 1992, kami ingin mengambil sikap untuk keunggulan dan kewirausahaan tanpa syarat, dan melawan hal yang bersifat pengecut dan rata-rata. Kami memulai nominasi kami hari ini dengan Ralph Dommermuth. Perusahaannya, United Internet, bernilai sekitar 11 miliar dan pada daftar kami untuk orang terkaya di Jerman, ia di peringkat ke-25 dengan kekayaan pribadi sebesar 4,5 miliar Euro. Secara kolektif para tamu pada malam yang elegan ini bernilai miliaran Euro. Inilah wajah kekayaan di Jerman: terutama pria. Dan meskipun kami dekat dengan orang super kaya di sini, dunia mereka entah bagaimana tetap tidak terjangkau. Hamburg, rumah bagi induk perusahaan Manager Magazine, Grup Spiegel. Hampir tidak ada publikasi yang melaporkan tentang orang-orang Jerman yang ultrakaya. Setiap tahun tim majalah itu mengumpulkan informasi tentang orang kaya Jerman dan - menggunakan model Forbes - membuat edisi khusus dengan daftar 1001 orang terkaya Jerman. Ini pekerjaan yang susah payah. Berapa miliarder yang ada di sana sekarang? 180 hingga 190? Tahun lalu 136, betul? Tidak. Tahun lalu ada sekitar 170. 170, oke. Siapa yang ingin masuk dalam daftar 1001 orang Jerman terkaya kami, perlu memiliki sekitar 100 juta. Itu tidak harus berupa uang di bank. Kebanyakan orang memilikinya sebagai aset atau sebagai properti. Tapi itulah stadion yang kami cari untuk berada dalam daftar orang-orang Jerman terkaya kami. Pemimpin redaksi Steffen Klusmann telah berada di sekitar orang Jerman ultrakaya selama bertahun-tahun. Apa yang diperlukan untuk masuk dalam daftar? Saya akan mengatakan, 150 tempat teratas dalam daftar kami akan selalu menjadi milik pemilik perusahaan dan ahli waris. Bahkan jika Anda seorang dokter kepala, Anda harus melihat banyak pasien untuk menjadi miliarder. Manajer juga mengalami kesulitan untuk mencapai setinggi itu. Di sini, di Jerman ada perdebatan tentang apakah kepala perusahaan DAX berpenghasilan terlalu banyak. Tapi jika Anda membandingkannya dengan orang-orang pada posisi serupa di AS, Inggris atau Cina dapatkan, itu sangat kecil. Jadi itu saja tidak akan pernah bisa membuat orang menjadi salah satu yang benar-benar ultrakaya. Membuat daftar orang terkaya, terutama sangat sulit di Jerman. Itu bukan karena jumlah mereka kurang. Dia entah bagaimana berhasil mengumpulkan kerajaan dan kekayaan besar ini hanya dalam beberapa tahun. Kekayaannya diperkirakan sekitar empat miliar. Dia adalah orang Jerman super kaya paling tidak dikenal. Apa dia akan membiarkan kita mewawancarainya di rumah? Mereka lebih suka terbang di bawah radar untuk menghindari rasa iri. Orang super kaya tidak suka membicarakan dirinya sendiri, begitu bisa dikatakan. Ya, Anda biasanya hanya bisa mendapatkan wawancara di rumah dengan mereka yang berada di liga kedua. Di sana Anda akan selalu mendapatkan orang yang menyukai publisitas. Seperti Bapak Maschmeyer misalnya. Mendapat foto bagus dari Bapak Maschmeyer tidak pernah menjadi masalah. Tetapi "uang" yang asli tetap menyembunyikan diri. Orang super kaya mencoba untuk tidak menarik perhatian. Kadang-kadang mereka bahkan mencoba bersembunyi. Beberapa orang tidak ada fotonya dalam daftar kami. Anda tak akan temukan satu gambar pun jika Anda pergi online dan mencari mereka di Google. Belum ada foto Reimann, keluarga terkaya Jerman, untuk beberapa dekade. Banyak yang tetap bersembunyi karena mereka ingin menjalani hidup normal dan berpikir mereka tidak akan bisa melakukan itu jika mereka dikenal sebagai multimiliarder. Selama berbulan-bulan permintaan wawancara kami ditolak, pengambilan gambar film yang disetujui dibatalkan pada menit terakhir. Tidak ada dari orang kaya yang mau berbicara dengan kami tentang uang. Akhirnya, kami beruntung di pusat keuangan Frankfurt. Di sini, di lokasi utama yang terletak di belakang menara-menara bank, terdapat perusahaan manajemen aset Focam. Focam mengelola kekayaan keluarga bisnis Jerman. Pimpinannya, Christian von Bechtolsheim, memberi sejumlah wawasan mengapa orang-orang Jerman yang kaya, sangat pemalu. Banyak orang Jerman yang kaya enggan melangkah keluar ke mata publik karena mereka khawatir mereka dapat dilihat secara negatif. Mereka bertanya pada diri sendiri: ‘apa yang saya peroleh dari menunjukkan diri kepada publik? Itu tidak memberi saya apa pun. Sebaliknya, itu bisa menyebabkan orang gila memperhatikan dan membobol rumah saya atau menculik salah satu anak saya.’ Dan ketakutan itu bukan tanpa alasan. Lalu, ada juga fakta bahwa banyak ahli waris yang mewarisi kekayaan, yang entah bagaimana dinodai oleh atau terkait dengan Jerman NAZI atau Reich Ketiga. Jajaran atas orang kaya Jerman menghindari publisitas seolah-olah itu wabah. Tapi apa yang begitu mereka takutkan? Saya bertanya kepada sejumlah teman saya apakah mereka mau diwawancarai untuk film ini, dan masing-masing mengatakan tidak. Kata mereka, ‘orang lain bisa melakukannya lebih baik dibanding saya, saya tak bisa melakukan dengan benar, saya mungkin akan membuat salah.’ Mereka pikir mereka memiliki lebih banyak kerugian daripada manfaat. Setelah banyak bolak-balik dengan tim persnya, seorang Jerman ultrakaya, setuju untuk bertemu kami. Michael Otto adalah Ketua Dewan Pengawas Grup Otto dan salah satu dari sepuluh orang Jerman terkaya. Kami bertanya kepadanya mengapa orang Jerman enggan memamerkan kekayaan mereka. Banyak yang khawatir hal itu akan menimbulkan kecemburuan. Di Amerika Serikat, prestasi dan kekayaan dilihat secara sangat positif. Tapi di sini mereka membawa sisa rasa pahit: ‘Dari mana kekayaannya berasal atau bagaimana dia memperoleh kekayaannya?’ Itu membuat sejumlah orang takut. Saya pikir banyak orang merasa kekayaan mereka sendiri agak memuakkan. Saya sama sekali tidak. Saya suka itu. Dirk Rossmann besar dalam kondisi miskin. Ibunya mengelola toko obat kecil di Hannover pasca perang. Putranya memiliki rencana yang lebih besar. Tahun 1972, gagasan untuk membuka toko produk kesehatan swalayan pertama di Jerman muncul padanya. Hari ini, dia multimiliarder. Mengapa dia senang menjadi sorotan? Pada tahun-tahun awal itu hanya tentang menempatkan nama Rossmann di luar sana. Jadi ketika saya diundang ke sebuah acara talk-show di acara regional kecil, saya suka pergi karena saya berpikir: "publisitas gratis untuk perusahaan saya". Tetapi dua atau tiga tahun yang lalu saya mulai memahami bahwa pria yang sedikit botak ini, yang giginya tidak sempurna, haus akan pengakuan bagi dirinya sendiri. Kala itu saya pikir saya memasuki pusat perhatian untuk mempromosikan perusahaan. Namun semua orang merasionalkan motif mereka, dan saya juga. Dalam empat puluh tahun, Rossmann menjadi rantai drugstore paling mencetak laba di Eropa, dengan toko-toko di enam negara. Pada 2018, total 56 ribu orang bekerja untuk rantai drugstore itu. Rossmann aktif di bidang bisnis lain juga, dan ia berspekulasi di pasar saham. Masukkan 10.000 lagi...Tepat. Batasnya 36,75 ... Oke, terima kasih. Sampai jumpa. Saya punya beberapa investasi ekuitas pribadi. Yang terbesar bernilai antara 80 dan 100 juta, tergantung pada harga bursa. Jadi itu angka yang adil. Dan kemudian ada juga andil saham... di berbagai industri. Untuk merinci itu semua akan membosankan. Anda masih masuk kategori UKM - Usaha Kecil Menengah. Mengapa menurut Anda begitu? Saya tidak begitu tahu. Dengan 55.000 karyawan, Anda bukan lagi perusahaan menengah. Anda berada di liga lain. Kami kembali ke Manager Magazine di Hamburg di mana foto-foto untuk edisi khusus sedang dipilih. Daftar orang-orang Jerman terkaya mencakup banyak pemilik perusahaan dari apa yang disebut bisnis kelas menengah. Ini adalah kekhasan Jerman. Dan itu bukan satu-satunya. Kisah sampul untuk topik saat ini adalah tahun lalu, terjadi hujan uang pada orang super kaya Jerman. Di atas sini kita memiliki Schaeffler, ibu pemimpin dan putranya: yang kini ia memiliki 80 persen, sang ibu hanya memegang 20. Schaeffler telah berada di puncak daftar kami selama bertahun-tahun. Saya harus memeriksa dengan tepat berapa nilainya, tapi sekitar 20 miliar, ditambah atau dikurangi sedikit. Di bawah sini kita memiliki Ibu Bagel-Trah, kepala klan Henkel. Ia orang nomor satu, dan sejauh ini satu-satunya wanita yang menjadi Ketua Dewan Pengawas pada satu dari 30 perusahaan DAX. Ekonomi Jerman masih sangat didominasi oleh pria. Yang menarik adalah jika Anda membandingkan daftar kami di sini, dengan daftar orang yang sangat kaya di Amerika Serikat, kami memiliki banyak uang lama; perusahaan lama yang telah berdiri selama beberapa dekade. Di Amerika Serikat, Anda memiliki semua pemuda - mulai dari Facebook, Google, Snapchat, dsb - yang telah menggelembung ke bagian atas daftar. Kami tidak memiliki hal semacam itu di sini. Dan dibandingkan dengan negara lain, Jerman sangat segan menunjukkan kekayaan mereka. Sangat sedikit orang Jerman yang berlayar dengan kapal seperti ini. Biasanya itu orang Amerika, Rusia, Cina, dan sebagainya. Di sini Anda tidak menunjukkan uang Anda. Anda mungkin punya berbagai rumah, villa, dan lainnya. Tapi paling mungkin ada Volkswagen yang diparkir di depan rumah. Tampaknya, rata-rata orang ultrakaya Jerman jarang mencolok. Tidak seperti di Amerika Serikat, atlet, aktor dan tokoh televisi, jarang masuk dalam daftar Jerman. Meskipun kami berusaha sepenuh hati dan tenaga memprediksi kekayaan ini, jumlahnya mungkin jauh lebih besar. Terutama jika kita berbicara tentang real estate perkotaan, di mana harga-harga telah meledak selama 10 tahun terakhir. Banyak orang menggandakan aset properti mereka. Jadi, jika Anda mulai dengan lima miliar di pasar itu, Anda mungkin memiliki 10 atau 15 miliar hari ini. Uang menghasilkan uang! Tapi sementara kekayaan orang Jerman kaya telah meledak sejak krisis keuangan - karena kenaikan nilai real estate, saham dan aset - mereka yang berpenghasilan rata-rata harus menelan kerugian. Bahkan lembaga ekonomi liberal pun prihatin tentang ketimpangan sosial di Jerman. Bagaimana orang super kaya melihat perbedaan ini? Judul berita di koran berbunyi: "Orang kaya semakin kaya dan semakin kaya." Yang adalah benar. Orang kaya semakin kaya. Itu adalah benar. Tapi itu juga salah. Ada 20 juta warga di Jerman, yang memiliki aset bernilai antara 100.000 hingga satu juta. Jadi jutaan orang menjadi semakin kaya. Sekarang, orang kaya semakin kaya bahkan lebih cepat, karena salah satu faktor mungkin mereka bisa mendedikasikan lebih banyak waktu untuk menambah kekayaan mereka. Sahra Wagenknecht dari partai Kiri mungkin akan berkata: "pisahkan kekayaan Anda!" Tapi respons saya adalah saya juga berbuat hal bagi dunia tempat saya tinggal, saya tidak hanya mengambil. Meskipun saya bukan seorang sosialis. Saya hanya dapat melakukan hal-hal karena saya memiliki 'hal-hal'. Michael Otto adalah salah satu orang kaya yang kekayaannya meningkat. Ia berhasil mengalihkan bisnis pesanan lewat paketnya menjadi perusahaan digital. Lebih dari dua puluh perusahaan sekarang milik grup Otto. Kami ingin tahu bagaimana Otto melihat debat tentang kaya dan miskin. Apakah yang kaya memahami kekhawatiran yang miskin? Saat orang-orang membahas orang-orang di atas yang tidak mengerti mereka yang di bawah, saya tidak akan mengatakan itu berlaku untuk saya. Karena saya tidak dilahirkan kaya. Saya datang ke Hamburg sebagai pengungsi dari Prusia Barat dan ayah saya harus memulai dari awal. Itu sebabnya saya benar-benar mengerti orang yang hidup dalam kemiskinan saat ini. Di sisi lain, saya juga mengatakan jika Jerman mendapatkan semakin banyak jutawan, artinya orang-orang dengan usaha kecil atau menengah - karena umumnya para jutawan itu membangun bisnis yang mereka jalankan sekarang - maka saya pikir itu hebat. Karena mereka adalah orang-orang yang menciptakan pekerjaan. Bagi saya, itu yang penting dalam perdebatan ini. Kita harus berfokus pada hal itu, dan bukan kaya versus miskin. Memastikan bahwa kekayaan keluarga kaya dapat meningkat meskipun tingkat bunga nol persen saat ini adalah misi perusahaan Christian von Bechtolsheim. ...Terima kasih atas undangannya. Untuk mengubah sedikit nuansa secara singkat... Setiap hari, ia dan karyawannya mengirimkan peluang investasi dari bank dan entitas lain yang diberikan secara eksklusif kepada kliennya yang kaya. Ini adalah cara untuk menyajikan penawaran kepada beberapa klien yang berharga dan Focam. Adilkah orang-orang kaya Jerman bisa menambah kekayaannya, sementara penduduk lainnya tertinggal di belakang? Saya pikir sulit untuk menerapkan istilah seperti kesamaan dan keadilan dalam hal distribusi kekayaan. Saya akan berkata, di sini di Jerman kami lebih baik daripada sebelumnya, dan orang-orang di sini hidup dalam keadaan di atas rata-rata. Bagaimana pun, kami harus memastikan kesenjangan antara kaya dan miskin tidak menjadi terlalu lebar, karena kami tidak ingin konflik sosial seperti di Amerika Serikat atau Amerika Latin terjadi di sini. Perusahaan von Bechtolsheim Focam, adalah apa yang disebut 'multi-family office'. Family office mengurus kebutuhan keluarga yang sangat kaya, mengelola dan meningkatkan aset mereka. Baru ada gunanya untuk menggunakan jasa family office jika kekayaan Anda berada di atas 30 juta Euro. Siapa yang mampu membayar hal seperti itu? Nah, family office seperti milik kami jelas tidak dapat membicarakan klien-kliennya. Kami memiliki keluarga-keluarga bisnis Jerman yang terkenal. Itu adalah profil khas klien kami. Seseorang yang adalah atau pernah menjadi pemilik perusahaan berpikir berbeda dengan seseorang yang menempuh hidupnya sebagai karyawan. Atau mereka adalah keluarga yang sudah lama memiliki uang, jadi kekayaan bisa dikatakan ada dalam gen mereka. Dan kemudian ada keluarga, yang baru saja memasuki kekayaan mereka - yang masih memompa dengan energi kewirausahaan. Mereka biasanya sangat berbeda dari ahli waris. Rainer Schaller tampaknya memiliki banyak energi wirausaha ini. Perusahaannya adalah McFit, rantai studio kebugaran terbesar di Eropa. Kantor pusatnya berada di pabrik roti tua di Berlin. Rainer Schaller memulai itu dari kecil. Tapi hari ini, kekayaannya bernilai 250 juta Euro. Saya lahir di dekat Bamberg dan besar di sebuah desa kecil. Biasanya di desa, Anda melakukan olahraga yang tersedia, dan ketika saya berusia sekitar 15, 16 tahun, panutan saya adalah Arnold Schwarzenegger dan Stallone. Itulah mengapa saya masuk ke dunia fitness. Schaller lulus sekolah menengah lalu menyelesaikan magang wiraniaga, dan menjadi manajer tiga supermarket. Kemudian dia memutuskan untuk memulai sesuatu yang baru. Ketika saya berusia 25 tahun saya memutuskan untuk beralih ke industri jasa kebugaran. Ide saya adalah membuka pusat kebugaran di mana semua orang bisa berlatih, terlepas dari penghasilannya. Itu ide awalnya. Dan saya punya tujuan besar. Saya ingin menjadi orang nomor satu di Eropa. Tapi hanya itu yang saya miliki, saya tidak memiliki biaya. Saat itu tahun 1997 ketika Schaller membuka studio kebugaran pertamanya di Würzburg dekat dengan desa asalnya. Ia memanfaatkan sejumlah metode yang tidak biasa. Würzburg adalah langkah besar bagi saya. Saya membuka studio kebugaran pertama saya di sana dengan slogan "Sekarang juga di Würzburg!" yang dilihat orang sebagai lelucon pemasaran. Karena konsumen datang kepada saya dan bertanya di mana lagi kami memiliki studio. Jadi saya memikirkannya dan membuat lelucon pemasaran berikutnya: "Segera juga di Erlangen." Itu berhasil dan juga menempatkan saya di jalur pergi dari Würzburg ke Erlangen. Dan kemudian itu berkembang dari sana. Sepuluh tahun kemudian Rainer Schaller mencapai tujuannya. Dia Nomor Satu di Eropa dan masih terus berekspansi. McFit sekarang memiliki 10 perusahaan jasa kebugaran serta agensi modelnya sendiri. Sementara itu Schaller bersiap-siap untuk membuka studio kebugaran di Amerika Serikat. Menjadi Nomor 1 jauh lebih mudah daripada tetap Nomor 1. Saya pikir jika Anda ingin sukses, Anda harus menjadi sedikit binatang alfa dalam diri Anda. Investor mungkin akan memilih merek dan berkata, “Oke, itu bisa berhasil. Saya suka,” meskipun mungkin dua langkah terlalu jauh bagi banyak orang. Tetapi jika seseorang tidak ingin terlibat dengan kami karena itu, apa masalahnya! Saya yakin, hal yang lebih mudah bagi Anda jika Anda telah berjuang sampai ke puncak dan meraih kesuksesan. Klien merasakannya, dan begitu juga mitra-mitra. Saya pikir itu situasi kami. Itulah sebabnya saya juga bisa membayangkan seseorang yang mewarisi sesuatu, atau mengambil alih - atau bahkan harus mengambil alih perusahaan pada generasi kedua atau ketiga - akan memiliki waktu yang jauh lebih sulit. Tetapi ada banyak ahli waris di Jerman. Kekayaan besar dan ribuan perusahaan telah diwariskan dari satu generasi ke generasi berikutnya. Ada ahli waris yang tidak mau, dan lainnya, yang tidak boleh mengambil alih bisnis orang tua mereka. Penerus memiliki makna sangat penting di antara mereka yang terkaya di Jerman. Michael Otto mewarisi perusahaan pengiriman barang lewat paket dari ayahnya dan secara sukses mengelolanya. Akan lebih sulit bagi anak-anak saya, karena kini Grup Otto memiliki 123 perusahaan di lebih dari 30 negara. Saya mengenal setiap perusahaan, apakah karena saya terlibat ketika itu didirikan atau karena saya memimpin negosiasi pengambilalihan. Tapi anak-anak saya belum mengenal banyak perusahaan. Anak-anak Otto memilih menentang penerusan secara langsung. Saya pikir penting untuk memberi anak-anak pilihan, tanpa menekan mereka, jadi Anda tidak memaksa mereka memasuki sebuah peran. Saya pikir kesalahan itu sering terjadi dan saya katakan itu buruk bagi anak-anak dan perusahaan. Jadi sekarang kita punya satu titik penjualan? Burgwedel? Apa kamu agak lelah? Ya. Sudah beberapa hari ini melelahkan. Dalam 10 menit saya akan fit lagi. Itu hanya 'bum, bum, bum'. Dirk Rossmann juga menghabiskan waktu lama mempertimbangkan siapa akan menjadi penggantinya. Itu sekarang sudah diputuskan. Raoul Rossmann, yang lebih muda dari dua putranya, akan mengambil alih sebagai pimpinan rantai drugstore. Raoul, apa kita menjual banyak benang wol? Itu bukan secara tepat bagian dari kisaran drugstore. Trennya sudah berlalu. Itu besar pada tahun 2016, sudah menurun tahun 2017, dan itu mengalami stagnasi pada 2018. Tapi bukan produk terburuk yang kita punya dan masih memasukkan sejumlah pendapatan. Ayah Raoul harus menunjukkan kepadanya daya tarik untuk menjadi penanggung jawab. Saat anak-anak itu semakin dilibatkan, saya pikir 'oh kini kita tidak bisa melihat seolah-olah bisnis drugstore hanya tentang menghasilkan uang.' Jadi saya tunjukkan kepada mereka kami juga aktif dalam masalah sosial, di Afrika dan sebagainya. Saya selalu tunjukkan pada putra-putra saya, apa yang kami lakukan bukan hanya menghasilkan uang, ini juga tentang bertanggung jawab atas orang lain. Ketika masih kecil saya ingin menjadi sutradara film. Itu selalu impian saya. Masih hari ini. Saya selalu berpikir, tidak, saya tidak ingin dia menjadi sutradara. Saya ingin dia ada di perusahaan... Jadi saya berkata: Raoul, silakan dan jadilah sutradara. Tapi menjadi direktur perusahaan besar juga menyenangkan. Jadi saya sedikit memanipulasi dia. Saya sering ditanya apakah saya merasa bersaing dengan ayah saya. Dan tentu ia membangun perusahaan besar ini yang hanya akan saya ambil alih. Tapi sangat sulit untuk mempertahankan sesuatu saat ini. Periode pendirian memiliki tantangan tersendiri. Memiliki gagasan untuk mendirikan drugstore swalayan saja sudah hal yang sangat inovatif. Tapi perjuangan untuk bertahan hidup semakin sulit, dan itulah yang saya alami. Semua orang di keluarga saya ingin handal dalam olahraga. Ayah saya dan saya saling bertarung dalam tenis. Kami semua saling bersaing. Dan itu juga membentuk pandangan kami tentang kehidupan, atau setidaknya pandangan saya. Terlepas dari persaingan mereka, keluarga Rossmann mencapai kesepakatan harmonis terkait masalah penerus. Bermain melawan Raoul cukup sulit bagi saya. Saya biasanya lebih suka bermain ganda, itu lebih cocok untuk usia saya. Saya mulai khawatir sedikit tentang dirimu. Kamu cemas tentang saya? Raoul, dengan warisanmu saya tidak akan khawatir. Saya merasa lega dengan itu. Memilih ahli waris dan penerus biasanya tidak cukup damai seperti pada keluarga Rossmann. Christian von Bechtolsheim telah melihat banyak perselisihan warisan dalam keluarga kaya. Adalah tugasnya untuk melestarikan aset keluarga dan melindunginya dari segala macam bahaya. Mempertahankan kekayaan keluarga selama beberapa generasi adalah sangat sulit, karena ia terancam terbagi-bagi melalui warisan, dari perselisihan kekayaan, dari pengambilalihan, dari perang, atau hanya dari kebodohan. Kebanyakan keluarga akan mengalami satu atau semua ini terjadi pada mereka. Hanya segelintir keluarga yang berhasil bertahan kurang lebih selama berabad-abad, tapi mereka yang berada di atas telah berganti berkali-kali. Von Bechtolsheim berbicara dari pengalaman pribadi. Sejarah keluarganya sendiri sudah ada sejak 900 tahun yang lalu. Saya punya nama sangat panjang, setidaknya pada akte kelahiran saya. Ada enam nama yang diberikan: Christian, Lothar, Ludwig, Hugo, Wilhelm dan Maria - sebagai seorang Katolik yang baik. Diikuti oleh "Baron von Mauchenheim genannt Bechtolsheim." Tapi di tempat kerja saya biasanya dipanggil Bapak von Bechtolsheim dan di acara sosial Baron atau Lord Baron. Baron von Bechtolsheim adalah penerus tidak langsung dari Fugger, keluarga terkaya dalam sejarah Jerman. Kapan ‘kekayaan’ dimulai menurut Anda? Jika Anda menanyai saya kapan saya menganggap seseorang benar-benar kaya, saya akan mengatakan 100 juta ke atas. Saya sudah jelas tidak kaya. Tapi saya baik dan keluarga kami nyaman dan saya sudah pasti tidak mengeluh. Ketika Anda memiliki riwayat keluarga sepanjang yang saya miliki, keluarga Anda telah melihat segalanya. Hampir bangkrut... Tahun-tahun dibanjiri uang... dan tahun-tahun ketika banyak yang hilang. Dia membawa kami ke pondok berburu keluarga Bechtolsheim di Thüringen. Dikira pondok itu akan hilang selamanya selama pembagian Jerman. Rumah itu dibangun pada tahun 1892 sebagai pondok berburu untuk paman buyut saya. Ini sudah milik keluarga sejak itu, kecuali untuk interval singkat. Itu diambil alih pada tahun 1952, dan kemudian dikembalikan lagi pada 1992, dan sejak itu, saya pemiliknya. Pondok berburu keluarga selamat dari perampasan dan sosialisme tanpa banyak kerusakan. Hari ini, von Bechtolsheim juga memiliki ratusan hektar hutan di dekatnya dan secara teratur mengundang rekanan bisnis untuk berburu. Banyak dari trofi itu milik saya, tetapi beberapa juga berasal dari ayah saya. Dan beberapa dari kakek buyut saya. Macan tutul itu di bawah sana, saya tidak menembaknya. Itu yang menembak kakek buyut saya dan kemudian anjing saya mengunyah telinganya. Jadi dia tidak lagi memiliki kecantikan sebelumnya, tetapi itu terlalu berharga untuk tidak disimpan. Topik yang berulang dalam edisi khusus Manager Magazine adalah jaringan kerja orang super kaya. Ada jaringan kerja lebih besar dan lebih kecil, dan ada banyak dari mereka, dan sebagian besar bahkan kami jurnalis pun tidak mengetahuinya. Di masyarakat kelas atas ada hobi khas tertentu: pacuan kuda, hoki, sedikit tenis - meskipun itu untuk generasi lama. Mereka akan bertemu dalam box di stadion sepak bola utama, karena tentu saja mereka semua adalah penggemar sepakbola. Sepak bola hal sangat penting. Banyak jaringan kerja terjalin di sana. Itu seperti tempat pasar mereka. Mereka bergaul dan bertemu di sana lebih daripada apa yang disebut pesta untuk orang kaya. Ah...bodoh, bodoh, bodoh. Ya, bagus! Kami selalu ada banyak karyawan di sini dan kami punya banyak teman dalam box. Christian Pfeiffer ada di sini hari ini, pakar kriminologi paling terkenal di Jerman. Kadang-kadang Jogi Löw atau seseorang dari bidang politik mampir. Christian Wulff dan Bettina adalah bagian lingkaran teman dekat saya, jadi selalu ada banyak hal yang terjadi di sini. Di Berlin, Rainer Schaller membuka klub baru. Cabang baru dari kerajaan fitness-nya. Schaller juga telah menjadi anggota jaringan orang-orang penting dari industri olahraga, bisnis, dan hiburan. Kita belum saling menyapa, bukan? Rainer, halo. Memang benar itu lingkaran tertutup yang sulit untuk dimasuki. Saya berada di posisi yang berbeda sekarang. Hai Rainer, nah bagaimana kabarnya? Lama tidak bertemu. / Apa rencana minggu ini. Saya akan ke LA. Selamat, saya dengar kamu sudah memutuskan. LA dan San Francisco, minggu lalu. Sekarang pelaksanaannya. GOL!!!!!... Dirk Rossmann siap meninggalkan box-nya. Dia ingin merayakan kemenangan dengan temannya, miliarder dan pemilik perusahaan alat bantu dengar Martin Kind. Kind juga salah satu pemilik dan presiden Hannover 96. Ruangan atau box-nya, terletak di ujung lain area VIP eksklusif. Saya sudah katakan, kita akan menang hari ini. Saya katakan kepadanya jika kita tidak menang, dia akan bayar 10 juta. Itu taruhannya, betul? Tidak! Saya tidak pernah mengatakan itu. Ini dia... dua pria setengah baya yang layak... Jaringan kerja penting untuk bisnis. Tetapi apakah orang kaya juga memiliki pengaruh terhadap politik? Bisakah pengaruh politik dibeli di Jerman? Cara terbaik yang tersedia bagi orang kaya atau super kaya di Jerman untuk mengerahkan pengaruh adalah jumlah karyawan yang bekerja di perusahaan mereka. Seseorang yang memiliki perusahaan dengan 100.000 karyawan, atau katakanlah lebih sedikit, mungkin 15.000 karyawan, dapat pergi ke asosiasi bisnis dan berkata: "Baik, silakan dan sahkan undang-undang itu. Tapi itu akan merugikan saya." Atau argumen yang lebih baik adalah "itu akan membebani Anda 2.000 pekerjaan di daerah itu." Tapi tidak ada orang super kaya yang secara teratur menelepon menteri atau Ibu Merkel dan mengatakan apa yang perlu terjadi selanjutnya, hukum pajak mana yang mereka inginkan dan sebagainya. Bukan seperti itu hal yang terjadi di Jerman. Saya pindahkan ke kanan lagi, karena kita masih belum tahu apa masuk akal menempatkan teks di sebelah kanan, karena terlihat lebih baik. Klasik, penuh gaya. Batas waktu untuk topik khusus semakin dekat. Intinya adalah peringkat dari 1001 orang terkaya Jerman. Apa pendapat orang kaya tentang peringkat ini? Peringkat itu adalah untuk hiburan. Mereka dikumpulkan bersama, kadang-kadang menggunakan kutipan pasar saham, tapi mereka tidak dapat diandalkan dalam cara apa pun. Saya tidak terlalu memikirkan peringkat ini dan saya tidak ingin dimasukkan, sebab ini menciptakan kesan orang kaya ibaratnya Paman Gober Bebek. Fakta bahwa saya berada di peringkat teratas, termasuk orang Jerman terkaya, itu membuat saya merasa bangga. Saya akan membaca Manager Magazine sekali-sekali, tetapi saya tidak pernah membaca peringkatnya. Saya tidak tahu apa saya ada di dalamnya, saya tidak memerlukannya. Saya punya tujuan lain. Salah satunya adalah Rainer Schaller kini ingin membuka pusat kebugaran terbesar di dunia di negara bagian Nordrhein-Westfalen. Mirai adalah bahasa Jepang dan berarti 'masa depan.' Kami pikir ini sangat cocok untuk seluruh konsep dan visi. Karena apa yang ingin kita buat di sini benar-benar unik dan belum pernah dilakukan sebelumnya. Tujuan kami adalah menjadi pusat kebugaran dunia. Schaller berpikir ia menemukan lokasi yang sempurna untuk mewujudkan visinya di Oberhausen. Dia menyewa sebuah kompleks pabrik tua. Saat ini, tempatnya masih digunakan untuk membuat bagian-bagian baja. Tapi tidak lama, ribuan pelanggan akan berolahraga di sini. Pintu keluar darurat akan ada di sini, kantor-kantor di atas. Schaller mengalami pengalamannya yang paling traumatis hingga saat ini di kawasan tersebut. Tahun 2010, 21 orang tewas dan 54 lainnya cedera dalam kepanikan massal pada Love Parade di Duisburg. Schaller telah menjadi penyelenggara parade itu. Penyebab kepanikan masih belum ditentukan secara pasti. Bagaimana seorang pengusaha di jalur cepat menghadapi tragedi semacam itu? Kejadian seperti itu akan selalu menghantui Anda, selama sisa hidup Anda. Saya mendapat tanggung jawab moral. Saya penyelenggaranya. Jika saya dapat memutar kembali waktu, saya akan melakukannya segera, dengan skala apa yang terjadi. Tapi Anda tak bisa membuatnya tak terjadi. Anda harus mencoba menghadapi apa yang terjadi. Dirk Rossmann juga telah mengalami kemunduran dan krisis. Pada tahun 90-an kami berekspansi secara dramatis ke Republik Ceko, Hongaria dan Polandia. Saya juga berspekulasi di pasar saham agak terlalu banyak, dan mengabaikan perusahaan. Kemudian tahun 1996, kami tiba-tiba rugi 12 juta Deutsche Mark. Dan bank tidak bercanda jika Anda berutang sangat tinggi dan kemudian Anda membuat kerugian besar. Itu sangat kritis. Dan kemudian saya mengalami serangan jantung tahun ‘96. Tapi semua orang tahu dalam hidup bisa terjadi kesulitan. Dan keadaan menjadi sangat sulit ketika itu. Jadi saya menarik mundur semuanya, termasuk spekulasi saham. Saya menjual semuanya dan berpikir: Satu-satunya yang penting sekarang adalah meningkatkan bisnis drugstore Rossmann. Itu adalah langkah yang tepat, untuk berfokus pada satu hal dan tidak melakukan banyak hal yang berbeda. Rossmann muncul dari krisis itu lebih kuat dari sebelumnya. Dia mulai berspekulasi lagi. Tapi, dia meyakinkan kami, hanya dengan kekayaan pribadinya. Mari kita lihat di mana Gazprom berada... Saya tidak punya laptop. Saya biasanya melakukan ini melalui teks NTV 254... ini dia. Dikatakan Gazprom berada di 3,75. Saya sudah bisa menjualnya sekarang. Saya membeli 250 ribu dari itu. Jadi 250.000 x 20 cent, itu akan memberi saya untung 50 ribu Euro. Tapi saya tidak akan menjual, saya pikir itu akan naik menjadi empat Euro... Kadang-kadang berhasil, kadang tidak. Tapi saya menikmatinya. tidak bermain lotre, karena saya rasa itu membosankan. Dapatkah kekayaan besar juga menjadi beban? Saya mengatakan bahwa bagi kebanyakan orang, meskipun mereka tidak akan secara sukarela memberikan uang mereka, fakta bahwa mereka ingin uang itu tumbuh dapat menjadi beban. Mereka dikendalikan oleh aset mereka sendiri. Misalnya untuk pindah ke Swiss atau ke suatu tempat untuk menghemat pajak, dan melepaskan seluruh lingkaran teman-teman mereka, dan pada dasarnya menjadi budak kekayaan mereka. Menurut pendapat saya, itu tidak masuk akal mengingat kondisi yang saat ini kami miliki di Jerman. Kondisi di Jerman saat ini lebih menguntungkan daripada sebelumnya bagi orang kaya. Mereka membayar pajak jauh lebih sedikit daripada yang mereka lakukan 25 tahun yang lalu. Hanya sebagian kecil yang masih merasa terdorong untuk beremigrasi ke luar negeri. Pasal 14 Konstitusi Jerman berbunyi: “Properti mensyaratkan kewajiban. Penggunaannya juga akan bermanfaat bagi publik.” Apakah orang kaya di Jerman memenuhi tanggung jawab ini? Saya pikir penting jika Anda berhasil, jika Anda cukup beruntung telah mencapai tingkat kemakmuran dan kekayaan tertentu, untuk memberikan kembali sesuatu kepada masyarakat. Bagi saya, itu sudah pasti. Michael Otto adalah salah satu donor terbesar Jerman. Uangnya membantu mendanai sektor lingkungan, budaya, dan sosial. Seperti kebanyakan orang kaya, ia lebih suka memutuskan sendiri untuk apa ia membelanjakan uangnya, daripada menyerahkannya pada negara. Otto seperti orang-orang kaya Jerman lainnya menyumbang jutaan untuk pembangunan Elbphilharmonie di Hamburg. Di Jerman, orang kaya suka menyumbang, dan ini membuat kontribusi penting bagi masyarakat dan kehidupan publik. Tapi secara umum, mereka menentang usul mendistribusikan kembali kekayaan lewat pajak lebih tinggi Jika bisnis akan memenuhi semua tuntutan dari katakanlah Ibu Wagenknecht dari Partai Kiri, maka jutaan orang akan bahagia dan segalanya akan baik untuk sementara. Karena jutaan orang akan memiliki lebih banyak uang. Tapi pembagian kembali kekayaan yang sejati tak pernah membawa keadilan sosial lebih besar dalam jangka panjang, tidak dalam sistem politik mana pun yang telah mencobanya. Saya tidak berpikir banyak tentang apa yang disebut "pajak orang kaya" karena dua alasan: Pertama, terminologi sendiri yang memberi stigma, dan kami di Jerman harus menghindari itu. Dan kedua, "pajak kaya" tidak akan ada gunanya. Sebaliknya: itu akan memotong tulang punggung ekonomi Jerman, karena orang kaya khas Jerman adalah pemilik bisnis menengah. Mereka menjadi tulang punggung ekonomi Jerman dan jika kami ingin menghancurkannya, kami tak bisa salahkan siapa pun selain kami sendiri. Edisi khusus siap. Hal-hal pada dasarnya tetap sama. - Who are the 'stealthy wealthy' and how do they make their money?: Kind: captions Language: en At present, the median net worth for somebody in their 20s, it's under $8,000. But too often, the perception of where and how to make the most money is media and personality influenced in television, film and music. We glamorize Wall Street and Silicon Valley, not middle America, but business ownership in Anytown, USA is increasingly the foundation for our very highest wage earners. As Juliet Chung of The Wall Street Journal recently reported. Many of them are distinctly boring and extremely lucrative, like auto dealerships, beverage distributors, grocery stores, dental practices, and law firms. That analysis was based on the work of Owen Zidar, a Princeton University economist, and Eric Zwick, an economist at the University of Chicago. Doctor Zidar will join me in just a moment. The two are soon to publish a book on this very subject. They studied anonymized tax data from 2000 to 2022. Their findings build off a previous academic paper for which they were coauthors, called Capitalists In the 21st century. The takeaway from both the prior and more recent analysis is that if we examine everyone in the United States and then focus on the top 10% of earners, for most, their main source of income is wages. A compelling takeaway from that book was that many of the millionaires profiled were not doctors, lawyers, or executives, but skilled tradesmen and small business owners. And you could say that that book has aged well. Its authors found that a significant portion of millionaires were self-employed in what might be considered blue collar or middle market industries. Think plumbers think electricians, contractors, or pest control operators. These individuals often built modest businesses over time. They exercised strict financial discipline and reinvested profits rather than indulging in lifestyle inflation. Unlike high income professionals who sometimes live paycheck to paycheck in high status neighborhoods, these tradesmen lived well below their means. They avoided debt. They focused on wealth accumulation rather than status signaling. - The Dark Secret Behind the Royal Family's Wealth | Empires of Dirt: Kind: captions Language: en-US Buckingham Palace has announced the death of Queen Elizabeth II, the longest-reigning monarch in British history. You might have heard— the Queen is dead, and the world is in mourning. Really quite surprised at how emotional I felt, actually. Sort of like a numb, empty feeling. Take down the bunting from the Jubilee, paint it black, and, respectfully, hang it up again. But respect doesn’t have to mean reverence, even if she was a bit like the nation’s nan. Many people find it hard to overlook the fact that the global significance and power of the British monarchy stems from the enslavement and exploitation of people of color. As the world remembers the most iconic moments from the reign of Queen Elizabeth II, it’s important to give equal attention to the human cost that made it possible. Royal Empires of Dirt— a show about how British monarchs got rich at the expense of everyone else. [HOW THE ROYAL FAMILY PROFITED FROM SLAVERY] If you look past the corgis, the coins, and the ceramic commemorative tat, you'll find that the royal family is a bona fide financial empire. Working out its exact value isn’t easy because, like any half-decent financial empire, it goes to great lengths to obscure it. Three cheers for the King. Hip hip— Hooray! —Hip hip! —Hooray! In early 2021, an investigation found that the Queen successfully lobbied parliament to keep her private wealth hidden from the public. But last year, Forbes estimated that the headline value of the royal family’s holdings was worth more than $28 billion. That includes Buckingham Palace, the Crown Jewels, huge areas of land across the UK, and some prime London real estate. The late Queen and Prince Charles did pay some voluntarily, but the British monarch is exempt from paying most taxes, including inheritance tax. That means Charles inherits her $737 million private estate without paying the 40% tax rate that the rest of us commoners do. Fire! The Crown of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland come to The Prince Charles Philip Arthur George. I am deeply aware of this great inheritance and the duties and heavy responsibilities which have now passed to me. Now that Charles is King, 1 billion pounds’ worth of property that belonged to him outright will also now pass to Prince William. It’s fair to say that the Windsors will not be weighing up whether to heat or eat this winter. But where did these riches come from in the first place? For it is the Empire, which includes one-quarter of all the land and of all the peoples of the world, that gives to Britain its position as a first-class power. From the 16th century onwards, the British monarchy was central to the establishment, expansion, and maintenance of the British Empire, the largest the world has ever seen. One of the ways they amassed wealth and power was through the transatlantic slave trade, of which the royals were largely in favor. The 1562 journey of John Hawkins, the first English person known to have included enslaved Africans in his cargo, was approved by Queen Elizabeth I herself. After Britain invaded Jamaica in 1655, the Royal African Company was set up by Royal Charter under King Charles II. Its purpose was to control the West African slave trade and protect the British monopoly against the Dutch. The RAC went on to transport more enslaved Africans to the Americas than any other single institution ever, lining the pockets of the Stuart monarchs. Many of those trafficked were branded with the initials DY, as in the Duke of York, who led the company and later became King James II. Britain’s shameful history of slavery is inextricable from the royals who were so eager to profit from it, and that barbaric legacy extends today. Historic royal residences like Kensington Palace and Hampton Court Palace that date back to the 17th century were, to some extent, funded by slavery. So while it’s hard to tell just how much of the Windsors’ wealth derives from slavery today, it quite literally helped to build the roofs over their heads. And these days, it’s the British taxpayers who are footing the maintenance bill. In 2014, they paid $7.6 million to refurbish a 20-room apartment for Will and Kate in Kensington Palace. During Queen Victoria's reign, the Slavery Compensation Act meant a 20 million pound payout, 17 billion pounds in today’s money, was paid out to slave owners, not the people who had been enslaved or the countries they were taken from. It represented, at the time, 40% of the Treasury’s annual spending budget. The sum was so large that it took British taxpayers 182 years to pay off. In recent years, more and more former colonies are saying they want out of the Commonwealth and an apology. And while they’re at it, their rightful share of those billions, maybe even trillions. It’s complex to calculate, but some estimated figures for reparations suggest that the UK owes a staggering amount of money— around $10 trillion to the Caribbean and to Africa each and $44.6 trillion to India. It's enough to make you sweat. Unless, of course, you’re Prince Andrew. Ahead of the couple touching down here, there have been protests which have cast a distinct shadow over this visit. Earlier this year, Will and Kate were embarrassed on their tour of the Caribbean, not just because of Will’s bad dancing. Before their arrival in Jamaica, over 100 public figures signed an open letter calling on the royal family and the British government to offer an apology and pay reparations. Prince William did express profound sorrow for the appalling atrocities of slavery, but that’s not actually an apology or a payout. Prince William, I hope you feel ashamed in some sense. They represent a certain kind of history that is unpleasant for our people. So you can see why so many people around the world view the Queen’s death as an opportunity to right historic wrongs. What remains to be seen is whether the new King will be open to reparations. But it’s unlikely they’ll come at a cost to him and his family. After all, they don’t call it “The Firm” for nothing.

- Time Management Tips for Homeowners: Kind: captions Language: en today we're talking about time management more specifically we'll discuss 10 ideas that can help you keep your house in good shape remember time management is not about being perfect it's about making the most of your time and keeping your house in good shape thanks for watching and happy home maintaining firstly establish a routine regular cleaning and maintenance tasks will prevent small problems from becoming big ones it's about making a habit of taking care of your home secondly prioritize your tasks not everything needs to be done at once identify what needs immediate attention and what can wait thirdly consider delegating tasks if you live with others share the responsibilities it can reduce your workload and teach others the importance of Home Maintenance our fourth idea is to use technology there are numerous apps and tools that can help you manage your time and tasks more effectively fifthly plan for the unexpected set aside time each week for unanticipated repairs or tasks this way you're not overwhelmed when something unexpected happens the sixth idea is to break down big tasks into smaller ones it's easier to tackle a large project when it's divided into manageable chunks our seventh tip is to set realistic goals overestimating what you can accomplish in a given time can lead to frustration be honest with yourself about what is achievable ninth remember to take breaks regular rest periods can improve your productivity and prevent burnout and lastly keep learning there are always new techniques and strategies for Home Maintenance and time management stay open to new ideas and continue to improve your skills

  7. @Kelly-o9c [1 likes]: Something that’s helped me a lot is keeping cleaning supplies in each room. I’m much more likely to wipe down the bathroom mirror or vanity as soon as I notice it’s dirty if the cleaner is right there under the sink. If I have to leave the bathroom, go downstairs, and grab supplies from the kitchen, I’m far less likely to do it. The same goes for my bedroom, I keep a few supplies in my closet so that if my mirror is smudged or my nightstand is dusty, I can clean it right away while I’m in the room.
Another thing that’s really helped is cleaning as I go. For example, when I’m cooking, I throw away empty boxes or wrappers immediately. By the time I’m finished, all the dirty utensils and dishes go straight into the dishwasher. After eating, I do the same thing, dishes go right into the dishwasher instead of piling up in the sink. It saves an extra step and keeps messes from building up.

Then initially we started letting it out mostly for near and dear ones and then I thought that key why don't I think about basically making this a sort of a semi-commercial proposition and that's when that idea of converting this into a business venture started basically when whatever little bit of advertising we do or the way we promote our farm stay it is based on two fundamental concepts one is getting that rural living experience in terms of hospitality we don't want them to expect the same kind of service which a typical hotel provides and second thing is enjoying the nature. I think the most important part uh taking a tour of the organic farm the way we have grown it. These are some of the experiences uh what we want our guests to enjoy and what we offer. >> When we get positive feedbacks from our uh guest that is the time uh we feel that yes we have done something right and that I think that is the major uh driving force to do uh these things. Actually as you know city life we are always busy uh doing something or the other. One task is completed, we are thinking about ne next uh thing. Here we can spend time with each other with we can spend time with nature. Also in city life we have got used to like ordering stuff from Swiggy, Zomato. Here we have to plan everything in advance like because the main shopping area is around 3 kilometers away from this place. So in older days we used to make list of monthly groceries and all. So like that we need to do it here. So also in city I think the temptations are there. I think going to a mall watching a movie those aimless wanderings in the mall end up you end up eating food which you should not eat, buy things which you don't need and at the same time when we come to visit our farmhouse there we spend time meaningfully in each other's company. I think you are trying to grow a tree, plant a new tree, look after the nature and the upkeep of the farmhouse. It's not as if we don't like city. Definitely we like city and the pleasure it offers. But I think what we find is we tend to slow down when it come to the farmhouse in terms of expenses also while we spend around 1 and a half lakh rupees in a city whereas it would cost around 45 to 50,000 if you live in a a farm >> normally just having a look around a farm like this tree has grown this much or this tree is giving so many fruits that is the best part actually to experience >> and another part I think you forgot to tell is plucking flowers >> actually during COVID when we stayed here. So I used to cook food and I used to tell my son or my daughter to get curry leaves or get coriander from the farm. So they used to happily do that because that was the thing they never experienced earlier. >> And also I think especially my elder son he's very picky when we go to go out to a restaurant if there is a mist going around in the restaurant he used to get very jittery and he will used to refuse I'm not going to eat in this place. street soil joint, same thing. But the same kid when he used to come to the farm, so he was happily helping me put fertilizers which also is made from manure and compost. So I see a quite a lot of change in the way he used to interact with nature in the past as well as after coming here in the farm. >> My mom is very proud of me. She thinks that I'm handling so many things. I'm handling this uh Anand Farms bookings, creating menus, attending guests and all my job, household responsibilities, my kids, education and all everything. She's really proud of him me. Yesterday also I told her about all these things interview and all. She had that those happy tears in her eyes. >> The way I plan things of course how things will happen I really don't know. Right now both of us have fairly good corporate careers going on. My younger daughter is in 12th standard. But once she graduates that is the time we would like to make a switch and then basically do something more on Anand farms. Currently my corpus is around 3 crores 33 3.25 crores out of which 1.2 crores is in direct equities. Uh 60 lakhs is in mutual funds around 1 1.25 25 crores is in the real estate house I which I own and there is around 50 lakhs in provident funds and NPS in terms of my retirement corpus which is obviously locked till we retire also around 50 lakhs I have kept aside in a children's fund which is for my elder sonishan and saraji for further education I think the financial independence is basically based on the three core pillars stability of income security of whatever investments we have that they are able to grow sustain ably without taking undue risks and basically safety of future income when we retire. For us financial independence doesn't mean that retire happily and do nothing. It is continuous contributions back to the society in any ways we can. Anan farms is not an escape. It is basically a creation contribution back to society and creating something which gives more purpose to the life. So when someone asks me about how do we go about building something like anundant farms I have two angles to look at it. One is basically make sure that your financial planning is in place and second is don't romanticize about it because you see the output uh correct but that output is there outcome is there after 12 to 13 years of our efforts but you need to be passionate about it you need to be hands-on because you just can't outsource some of these things you'll have to be there on the ground and you'll have to do it in phases because you can't say that I will save 1 k and suddenly buy a land and construct a house within a year It doesn't go like that. So you'll have to plan it and in a phased manner that you buy the land first, make it plantation worthy. Make sure the resources like water, electricity are available in the area where you are planning to buy the land and then obviously enjoy the experience because I think most importantly that's what it is all about right it is not about getting that house ready and start living in it but the whole process you need to enjoy. Very touching and emotional instance. So we have a two families who visited us I think two and a half uh 3 years uh back and one of them has a had a pet dog called called as Zoe and she used to enjoy whenever they used to visit with her to this farm she used to roam freely and enjoy the place and when she died so they wanted to sort of plant a tree in her memory so they have done that actually in front of Anand farms that is another testament of how people love their experience with an farms Hey, if you think you resonate with these stories and you think you have one worth sharing too, then please fill in the form in the pin comments below and you can get featured on Winalth.

Under the leadership of CM Neiphiu Rio, Nagaland has launched some such schemes which have made a significant difference in the development of Nagaland. The first of the major initiatives is the Chief Minister Health Insurance Scheme, which provides health insurance cover to all citizens. State Capital Improvement Programme in which infrastructure plans are being made in Kohima and Dimapur. School education transformation in which digital classes and e-learning are being provided. Financial aid is being provided to farmers under the Agriculture and Horticulture Mission. Through the Field Youth Program, youth are being made self-reliant and self-empowered. Nagaland, nestled in the mountains, has always faced connectivity issues. But now some such schemes and projects have been launched which are improving connectivity. Some of those plans include major upgrade of NH29. Modernization and expansion of Dimpur Airport , mission to provide electricity to every village and vision to make every village digital through Bharat Net. With small steps but strong direction, Nagaland is today becoming a hub of entrepreneurship. Small handicrafts and handloom industries are also getting a boost. Local investors are getting funding and eco- friendly goods are also being exported. Nagaland was earlier called the Land of Festivals. Today it is also being recognized for its tourism in the country. Hornbill Festival is a grand festival of cultural and tradition celebration. Eco tourism and green villages like Djoko and Kohama are attracting tourists. Activities like tracking, campaigns and word watching are being done. A Cultural Hub for the Preservation and Promotion has also been created. Along with nature, Nagaland is also paying full attention to environmental conservation. Community forests are being saved. Awareness is being created about some rare species and their safety measures are also being taken. Solar street slides and home solar systems are being installed and several initiatives are also being taken for waste management. The resolve to convert all challenges into opportunities is Nagaland's vision for 2030. The picture of Nagaland will become clearer in the coming years. There will be 100% digital governance. Green energy hubs will be created. Global tourism will be promoted and heritage conservation will be done. Nagaland is no longer just a symbol of culture and diversity but has become a symbol of new thinking, new identity and self-reliance. So we had to be very careful of uh how much to spend. Internal valley logically made a a lot of sense because land prices were muted, not as high as the rest of the state. And for a sustainability of agriculture, water availability is not necessarily a criteria. But because we were new to farming, we didn't want to throw in too many risk factors and you know have some controllables at least and thanks to a perennial river Tamir Bernini uh throughout there it gets both branches of monsoon southwest uh as well as uh northeast. We bought this land in 2018 uh and the land cost uh 65 lakhs roughly. We built the house in 2020. We started in 2019 and finished in 2020. roughly with the house and the land put together a little less than a cr is what we spend. So we are right now at a break even stage. So right now for the last 6 months we have sustained around 40,000 profit after all expenses uh per month we are generating profit from the farm the online store and the farm stay put together. While I was working uh with ITC and housing.com I was spending just for myself around 40 to 50,000 on average every month. Whereas now for me, Noshi and Vana put together in this farm life, we barely spend 20,000 every month. Overall, the difference must be at least five times lesser in the farm life compared to our city life for sure. My portfolio size roughly is around 80 lakhs. Right now it was at 80 lakhs when we started this farming journey and it still has I think it has grown by a couple of lakhs not more than that. That's the major reason is because I've taken a lot of funds and used it towards infrastructure building and building the business uh marketing expenses and trials and errors with a lot of uh products that we are doing. So when I started out it the debt to equity ratio must have been 50/50. I was much riskier then also because I was younger and I I had a salaried job. She's actually running the farm now. I'm trying to run the business side of things, the revenue side of things. So later when we started talking to people around we realized that there are several people like us who have like quit their careers and moved back to villages and we found a network of 10 15 people and that's when things started getting getting easy. I don't miss living in the city at all. I like the peace that I get here but the only thing that I miss is the convenience of living in a city. Sometimes I miss that. I cannot spend more than 2 days in a city right now. So, I'm definitely much more comfortable, convenient with what I have here. But sometimes the convenience that you have in a city, I miss it. Right now, if you ask me, my goal is to be able to grow as much food that I can with the land that I have and uh feed that to my little family that I have here. feel you deserve to earn that's the first level of Financial Freedom uh the second level is when you feel you can spend the way you want I think that's the second stage of Financial Freedom and the third stage is when you can give it away you can help somebody else with that money without feeling tight fisted about it and we achieved that and that's why this whole transition and life change we worked on my own startup so for us we had already started thinking of an alternate life or we had already started thinking about a life where we did not have that paycheck and we were quite comfortable about that I think maybe our parents generation still comes from that mindset that you know if you're getting more why don't you take it if if you can earn for 20 more years why will you stop earning today so what if you have much more money and many more houses and things like that but I think um we we don't have the same kind of perspective so for example I think the time that we've spent with our daughters before they've left for college is far more valuable than another piece of property or another mutual fund deposit or some something else somewhere else and it's just a question about what you value in life and what you don't when people make a very sudden move to the countryside what happens is that they don't understand the countryside and they tend to carry their urban lifestyle into the village so they'll buy a piece of land the first thing they want to do is build a house centrally air conditioned 10,000 square ft swimming pool this that it's at complete odds with the village so what have you done you've actually done a disservice to the environment and to the people here so when you decide to come here you have to adopt the lifestyle also and not just the benefits you also have to integrate into the community you have to speak in the local language you have to attend the local weddings the local funerals you have to get involved in their lives Suk you have to be part of that you can't uh lead a isolated City rich person life in a poor Village you have to really integrate with them when we decided to finally start building the house and moving into Ananda as our primary and permanent residence we knew that we couldn't build a conventional cement and steel house because that would be an insult to the ecosystem that we had built so our house is made of ramed Earth stone and lime when we were building this house we wanted to build something that would last at least 100 years so that we are not burdening our children with the expense and the effort of renovating this house constantly building a house like this costs as much much as a normal house would in terms of absolute amounts but the difference is that in a normal modern house you probably spend 70% of the budget on materials and 30% on labor but in a house like ours we've spent 70% of our budget on labor and 30% on material and you know we are happy that we've put money into people's pockets instead of blowing it up on materials it took 20 months to build and I know that during that time many of the people who worked on our house earned enough money to build a house of their own in the village and so for us this is a way for us to sort of transfer the wealth back into the countryside back to the poorer sections back to uh smaller towns and boost that economy I think City life is great while you're earning and going to an office if you're not earning in the city it is my personal belief that you should not be living there we need to make way for other people to live that will help reduce the overcrowding that we are seeing in these cities in fact instead of doing this I find that a lot of people are bringing their parents from small towns to big cities even when we used to live in B Bombay like many times we would uh invite our parents to come see us and my father I remember used to feel extremely claustrophobic in my house he used to sit in the balcony all the time and that time I used to feel a little offended you know that what is this you're coming to my house and feeling claustrophobic but now I understand what that feels like we feel we have the most luxurious lifestyle we feel this is real wealth being surrounded by Clean Air ask people who live in GGO who live in Delhi who live in Noida what is clean air and how much value that has you know ask people who are now eating contaminated food and suffering from cancer that what is the value of clean food these are invaluable things again the way we socialize with people has changed in Mumbai we would meet larger groups of people over brightly lit evenings now we meet smaller groups of people in dimly lit evenings in fact we've probably had more friends visiting us here in the last 2 years than in the last 20 years so it's been really really wonderful and I doubt if I was living in the city that we would have had so many visitors who would come and spend you know 2 3 days with us uh at a stretch so when we bought this land it was a Barren degraded piece of land so in 2010 we actually learned about permaculture from a friend and when we heard about permaculture that's when we thought that this was something we could do permaculture stands for permanent agriculture which means how can we create permanent human settlements which are more stable which don't degrade the environment that they live in and which will give us all the resources that we need for ourselves but at the same time not ravage the environment around us so how can we take without depleting we grow about 120 to 150 food crops starting from cereals like wheat and millets we grow all pulses All dlls We Grow probably about 50 60 vegetables across winter and summer we grow all our own spices we also grow oil seeds like peanuts Sesame and sunflower so we have our own source of oil so our philosophy is we grow what we eat and we eat what we grow if we are not able to grow something we assume that it is not the right food for our bodies you know till the time that we were working I think we were so busy working we had no time to manage our investments 2016 my husband stopped working so he had more time on his hands and then he could really start looking at our own financial planning a little bit better and I think he's arrived at the best sort of portfolio mix which gives us both um long-term gains as well as short-term liquidity 40% of our savings would be in real estate 35% of our um wealth would be in equity mutual funds 15% would be in debt mutual fund and about 10% would be in Gold so the 40% which is in real estate also includes Ananda and that is giving us a return on a daily basis as well because we use this land to produce our own food and uh it's our home for everybody who thinks that they are going to buy a piece of land in the countryside start growing food and then sell that food and make some money let me share my experience what I have realized is that there is no money in selling food and the reason being that that we live in a poor country where food will always be cheap so what I recommend to people is to always diversify their revenue streams when they take on a project like this and by all means keep your surplus food as one of the streams of income but not the only one so what I've tried to do here at Ananda is actually I have 7 eight streams of income I'm a seed saver so I have a seed bank of my own and I actually run an online seed shop so that's one of my sources of income people come for permaculture tours that's my second source of income I run sustainable House tours I run Farm stays here I do online consultations for people who are designing their own permaculture Farms or building their own sustainable homes I uh run permaculture courses online and I run a YouTube channel which also gets me money from advertisers so that's my seventh uh source of income and then of course I sell my food so I actually have eight buckets of Revenue and that is what helps me to get enough money to run this farm and to make this whole project self-sufficient so Ananda has been built over 10 years with a lot of effort from not just me my husband and our children but also a lot of the villagers who joined this project and became part of our Farm staff so our children have actually worked on this land from the time that they were 7 and 10 so they have grown up with Ananda and they have a very strong sense of bonding with this place which is different from projects that people take on once their children have left home I meet a lot of lot of people who say this is my retirement plan I'm going to do it when I retire but what happens then is that the children are no longer part of it and when you die the kids have only looked at it as a piece of land that was bought by their parents and they can only think of selling it because they have no emotional attachment to it so I strongly encourage people to start a project like this in parallel to your day jobs firstly that time you're earning incomes so you don't worry about expenses that much you know you can afford things at retirement you only have a passive income you don't want to take on large scale projects that time because you might dip into your savings if today I had to walk into the land that I had walked into in 2010 I don't think I would have had the energy to do the same work so a lot of times when people do financial planning they keep in mind that they need to leave a inheritance for their children I mean I hear this all the time you know or you are buying jewelry or you're buying you know things to give to your children what we forget is if our children cannot breing clean air if they can't drink fresh water if they don't have unpolluted food to eat what are they going to do with all this money you know one of the things my kids tell us mommy Papa we know you did your best okay so we know the world's going to be tough for us but we know that you've done your best so you don't feel guilty about anything and that's a big thing thank you for watching and supporting our Channel if you like the stories we tell here's your chance to be a part of it if you or anybody you know has an interesting Financial journey to share then please fill the form in the pin comment below and we'll get in touch with you

- What Is Risk Management For A Family Office? - The Rich And Famous Files: Kind: captions Language: en [Music] What is risk management for a family office? Imagine having a treasure chest filled with your family's wealth and legacy. Now, think about all the ways that treasure could be at risk. That's where risk management steps in to protect what matters most. For a family office, which handles the financial affairs of wealthy families, risk management is about spotting potential dangers before they become problems. It involves looking at everything from investments to legal issues and even personal safety. A family office creates a clear set of rules and structures to guide decision-m. They often write a family constitution or bylaws that spell out who makes decisions, how disputes are resolved, and what the family's core values are. This helps prevent disagreements that could threaten the family's wealth or harmony. They also set up oversight committees like investment, treasury, and philanthropy groups to keep an eye on different areas. These committees make sure decisions are transparent and risks are watched closely. Regular reviews are a big part of risk management. Family offices check for risks related to taxes, legal compliance, and potential fraud. They also keep an eye on changing laws and regulations that could impact the family's assets. For celebrities or high-profile families, risk management also involves protecting personal information from cyber threats and identity theft. They often set up secure systems to safeguard sensitive medical data and financial details which can be targets for hackers. Managing investment risk is another key part. Family offices diversify investments across stocks, bonds, real estate, and private equity. They continuously monitor these investments and make adjustments as needed. This helps keep the portfolio balanced and resilient against market swings. They also plan for emergencies by setting aside rainy day funds that grow tax-free and can be accessed quickly if needed. This is especially important for families with unpredictable income streams or high lifestyle expenses. Reputation is everything for wealthy families and celebrities. Risk management includes controlling what information gets out and advising on employee issues. This helps protect their public image and avoid costly legal problems. For example, they might restrict access to certain personal details or carefully vet staff and service providers. In essence, risk management for a family office is about combining traditional financial controls with tailored strategies that fit the family's unique lifestyle and needs. It's a proactive approach that balances growing wealth with shielding it from threats. This way, the family's assets and legacy stay secure for generations to come.

- How to Build Generational Wealth (IMPORTANT): Kind: captions Language: en number 10 set up a trust throughout history&nbsp; wealthy families have devised clever strategies&nbsp;&nbsp; to maintain control and preserve their fortunes&nbsp; to name a few the Asters and Vanderbilts are there&nbsp;&nbsp; let's look at how trust Works assume you have&nbsp; a family Corporation but instead of running a&nbsp;&nbsp; business you place all your assets in this trust&nbsp; the fascinating part is that while you no longer&nbsp;&nbsp; technically own the money you still retain&nbsp; complete control and that's not all once your&nbsp;&nbsp; wealth is within the trust you can invest it as&nbsp; you see fit your net worth may appear modest say&nbsp;&nbsp; 15 million dollars on paper however considering&nbsp; the numerous trusts holding your assets your&nbsp;&nbsp; wealth could exceed 150 billion the beauty of&nbsp; this Arrangement is that your money is safe&nbsp;&nbsp; because it no longer belongs to you directly&nbsp; so if you face a lawsuit or other financial&nbsp;&nbsp; difficulties the assets of your trust remain&nbsp; Untouchable just as the funds of a corporation&nbsp;&nbsp; cannot be accessed through the founders accounts&nbsp; another benefit of trusts is their ability to help&nbsp;&nbsp; your children avoid the burdensome death tax so by&nbsp; passing on your wealth through the trust your kids&nbsp;&nbsp; can effortlessly step into the role of Trustees&nbsp; upon your passing the benefit is twofold they can&nbsp;&nbsp; gain control over the assets while minimizing&nbsp; tax liabilities number nine life insurance&nbsp;&nbsp; life insurance policies are pretty simple you&nbsp; pay a monthly premium to the insurance company&nbsp;&nbsp; and when you die they pay a predetermined sum&nbsp; to your family however the super rich have&nbsp;&nbsp; discovered that they can modify their policies&nbsp; to be similar to supercharged bank accounts with&nbsp;&nbsp; additional tax benefits they can invest far more&nbsp; money in their policies than ordinary citizens&nbsp;&nbsp; and this money can grow tax-free it's like having&nbsp; a secret investment account the government can't&nbsp;&nbsp; touch not only that but their funds are&nbsp; also safe from creditors and legal issues&nbsp;&nbsp; but here's the kicker they can borrow money from&nbsp; their policies to invest in other things assume&nbsp;&nbsp; you have a life insurance policy with a cash value&nbsp; of one million dollars you can borrow a hundred&nbsp;&nbsp; thousand dollars against that cash value at a&nbsp; lower interest rate than you could from a bank&nbsp;&nbsp; the money can then be invested in something with&nbsp; a higher return such as the stock market which&nbsp;&nbsp; typically yields around seven percent per year&nbsp; now this is where things really get interesting&nbsp;&nbsp; even though you borrowed a hundred thousand&nbsp; dollars the insurance policy's cash value of one&nbsp;&nbsp; million dollars continues to grow at its rate so&nbsp; while you're borrowed one hundred thousand dollars&nbsp;&nbsp; is earning seven percent in the stock market&nbsp; your one million dollar cash value is earning&nbsp;&nbsp; its interest say five percent that means that you&nbsp; earn seven percent on the hundred thousand dollars&nbsp;&nbsp; borrowed and five percent on the remaining&nbsp; nine hundred thousand dollars in the policy&nbsp;&nbsp; the result you end up profiting from the&nbsp; difference between the interest rate you&nbsp;&nbsp; pay on the loan say three percent and the rate&nbsp; of return on the borrowed money seven percent&nbsp;&nbsp; this is referred to as an Arbitrage opportunity it&nbsp; simply means that you benefit from the different&nbsp;&nbsp; prices or interest rates it's a clever&nbsp; strategy that the ultra Rich have used&nbsp;&nbsp; for years and now you know their secret too&nbsp; another interesting thing they do is set up&nbsp;&nbsp; life insurance policies for their children and&nbsp; grandchildren when they pass away the money from&nbsp;&nbsp; these policies goes back into the family ensuring&nbsp; that future Generations have Financial Security number eight invest your money creating&nbsp; generational wealth is an exciting inventure&nbsp;&nbsp; that requires a little bit of patience and clever&nbsp; thinking remember that it won't magically happen&nbsp;&nbsp; overnight or in a single generation but don't&nbsp; worry even if you don't have much money you&nbsp;&nbsp; can still set the stage for generational wealth by&nbsp; passing your financial success to your loved ones&nbsp;&nbsp; forget about the traditional approach of Simply&nbsp; handing over the money through a will which&nbsp;&nbsp; often results in a one-hit wonder that Fades away&nbsp; quickly to make a lasting impact you must improve&nbsp;&nbsp; your game and ensure your wealth thrives for&nbsp; future Generations the key is to go for long-term&nbsp;&nbsp; investing once your family has built a solid&nbsp; Financial Foundation focus on low-risk investments&nbsp;&nbsp; that guarantee stable growth then comes the fun&nbsp; part diversify your Investments combine long-term&nbsp;&nbsp; Financial Ventures with tangible assets unlike&nbsp; volatile stocks or bonds physical assets offer&nbsp;&nbsp; more stability consider investing in valuable&nbsp; properties like land or commercial real estate&nbsp;&nbsp; yes some upfront capital is required but there is&nbsp; also less expensive options such as gold bars or a&nbsp;&nbsp; collection of rare books but wait there's more you&nbsp; can even go for unconventional Investments like&nbsp;&nbsp; fine art or collecting Treasures these assets can&nbsp; appreciate over time ensuring your generational&nbsp;&nbsp; wealth remains Rock Solid number seven buy&nbsp; borrow die the buy borrow die strategy is a&nbsp;&nbsp; clever approach that promises to help you avoid&nbsp; heavy capital gains taxes and potentially leave&nbsp;&nbsp; a financial Legacy for Generations this strategy&nbsp; is popular among billionaires and Savvy investors&nbsp;&nbsp; for all the right reasons it involves a unique&nbsp; combination of buying borrowing and strategically&nbsp;&nbsp; planning for the end game here's how it works&nbsp; first you purchase appreciating assets such as&nbsp;&nbsp; stocks real estates or other Investments with&nbsp; the potential to increase in value over time&nbsp;&nbsp; but here's the twist instead of selling these&nbsp; assets and incurring significant capital gains&nbsp;&nbsp; taxes you borrow against their value to see The&nbsp; Magic In Action suppose you bought a prime piece&nbsp;&nbsp; of real estate a few years ago and its value has&nbsp; skyrocketed instead of selling it and paying a&nbsp;&nbsp; hefty tax bill you take out a loan against the&nbsp; property's equity this will provide you with&nbsp;&nbsp; the required funds without incurring capital&nbsp; gains taxes you now have the cash to invest&nbsp;&nbsp; in additional assets or enjoy the fruits of your&nbsp; labor the most genius part of the strategy lies&nbsp;&nbsp; in the final step when you die your errors inherit&nbsp; the assets at their current market value erasing&nbsp;&nbsp; any potential capital gains tax liability isn't&nbsp; it amazing the buy borrow die strategy enables&nbsp;&nbsp; you to leverage the growth of your Investments&nbsp; access funds when needed and ultimately leave a&nbsp;&nbsp; significant financial Legacy to your loved ones&nbsp; while minimizing the burden of capital gains&nbsp;&nbsp; taxes remember this strategy has complexities&nbsp; and potential risks so do your homework first&nbsp;&nbsp; number six have multiple income streams do not&nbsp; rely just on one source of income instead you&nbsp;&nbsp; have to develop multiple income streams working&nbsp; earn you a higher income become an expert in a&nbsp;&nbsp; specific field or industry and let your expertise&nbsp; open doors to better paying opportunities&nbsp;&nbsp; lastly remember to reinvest your earnings&nbsp;
